programme.desc: A newlywed couple's honeymoon is disrupted by a friends' marital problems.

programme.title: Out West With the Hardys
programme.desc: A judge takes his family out West to help a friend negotiate water rights.

programme.title: Ben-Hur
programme.desc: A rebellious Israelite prince crosses paths with Jesus Christ.

programme.title: The Super Cops
programme.desc: Two rogue cops enlist a streetwalker to help them stop a deadly drug ring.

programme.title: Bad Day at Black Rock
programme.desc: A man seeks for a Japanese American farmer to whose son he owes his life.

programme.title: The Music Man
programme.desc: A con man falls in love and stays when he should take the money and leave.

programme.title: Ride the High Country
programme.desc: Two aging gunslingers sign on to transport gold from a remote mining town.

programme.title: Soldiers Three
programme.desc: Three British officers look for adventure in 19th-century India.

programme.title: Onionhead
programme.desc: A lazy student enlists in the Navy expecting to sit out World War II.

programme.title: Jim Thorpe: All American
programme.desc: The famous athlete fights prejudice in his pursuit of sports stardom.

programme.title: Andy Hardy's Double Life
programme.desc: A new college student lets romance get in the way of his studies.

programme.title: The Belle of New York
programme.desc: A turn-of-the-century playboy courts a Salvation Army girl.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Angela and Brennan are caught in a melee at a hip-hop club, when a wall breaks open and reveals a mummified corpse and a stash of drugs.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Brennan and Booth race the clock to tie up loose ends before the defendant in Booth's first FBI case is executed for the crime.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: A girl's decomposed remains are found in a refrigerator, leading Brennan to confront her former mentor.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: While trying to identify remains from the 1950s, Zack cuts into a bone and accidentally releases deadly fungus spores, causing the whole team to be quarantined in the lab over Christmas.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Extensive plastic surgery hides the identity of a body whose parts were found near an airport.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When a woman's burned body is found in a car with evidence that her child was kidnapped, Brennan and Booth's investigation leads to a suspect who is in the Witness Protection Program.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: After witnessing the death of an MMA fighter, Toby looks into the business dealings of two rival leagues.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: Toby's investigation into the murder of a tattooed woman leads him to believe that corporate espionage and blackmail are behind the killing.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: The murder of a British spy may be linked to an adulterous dating site.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: Toby and Michelle team up with a con artist to catch a human trafficker.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU team investigates when the remains of a dozen people are discovered along a creek bed in Yakima, Wash.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: While conducting a profiling course on his birthday, Rossi gets a call from serial killer Tommy Yates, who gives him the location of his latest victim.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team investigates when home invasions involving casualties strike upscale neighborhoods in San Diego.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates when a bizarre hieroglyph is found under a deceased homeless man's armpit; Reid decides to take his mother out of a clinical study early to bring her home.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU team is called in to investigate when male bodies are discovered that seem to be impossible to identify.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: When a BAU member gets in trouble in Mexico, the team enlists the services of the International Response Team for help.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU looks into the cause of several fatal car crashes involving drivers and pedestrians; Reid gets bad news.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates a series of disfiguring acid attacks on young men and women; Dr. Reid begins to settle into his new surroundings.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A mother steps forward with valuable information for the BAU to aid in their investigation of an unsub known as the bone crusher; Reid must adapt to a new set of rules in prison.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU believes two unsubs may be operating simultaneously when victims killed in two different ways are discovered in the same city; the team grapples with keeping Reid safe in prison.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates an urban vampire who kidnaps his victims at night and holds them underground in the sewer system; Reid makes a difficult decision in prison.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU is called to investigate after three victims are discovered tied to stakes in the brutal Arizona desert.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates when several federal government employees, including a close friend of Walker's, succumb to symptoms of a heart attack; Reid anxiously awaits his trial date.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: When Reid fears his mother has been abducted, he asks the BAU to investigate while he's stuck in prison.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: Morgan brings the BAU new information in the case against Mr. Scratch, a serial killer and escaped convict who has been wreaking havoc on the team.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: Agent Matt Simmons joins the BAU team in a race to take down Mr. Scratch and save one of their own in the process.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates three similar crimes in which victims' remains are discovered in old suitcases.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: When an anger-driven, unknown subject targets successful businessmen in Detroit, the BAU must investigate.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU investigates a workplace shooting committed by a state-of-the-art drone in Silicon Valley.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: Morgan returns to the BAU to help Penelope deal with a case that has affected her emotionally.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: While helping a friend in Puerto Rico, MacGyver is taken hostage by robbers who are attempting to steal $2 million from the bank his friend manages.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and Riley are called in to examine a mysterious object that crash-landed in the Nevada desert; Riley uncovers new information about Mac's father but questions whether to deliver it to him after he says he needs to move on.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver gets a shocking surprise when he tries to tell Matty he's quitting the Phoenix Foundation.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Three months after quitting the Phoenix Foundation and moving to Nigeria, Mac puts aside his differences with his father and returns when he hears Jack is the target of a murderous dictator he double-crossed.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Jack receives an emergency distress signal from one of his old Delta buddies who has been accused of terrorism in a foreign country; MacGyver and Jack quickly recruit the rest of Jack's old Delta team and stage an unsanctioned rescue operation.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver and the team go under cover on a college campus to find an operative who is recruiting students to carry out deadly attacks; Jack joins bounty hunter Billy Colton in the hunt for a bail jumper.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver and Riley must find a way to drive a tanker filled with liquid oxygen through a region occupied by rebels and deliver it to a children's hospital; Riley helps MacGyver decide whether or not to allow his father back into his life.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and Jack rush to Mexico to help capture the leader of a drug cartel while the country celebrates Dia de Muertos.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Unable to relax on their honeymoon, Booth and Brennan decide to help a local detective solve the murder of a Nazi war criminal; Hodgins and Angela look after Christine.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team suspects that a man who made his money donating sperm was killed by a recipient who learned his records were inaccurate; Brennan enters a feud with a fellow author (Nora Dunn).

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: A key witness turns up dead in a case for which Brennan is a juror, so the team works to discover what has happened.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a food scientist whose body was put through a meat grinder and mixed into canned stew; Angela puts together a post-wedding bachelorette party for Brennan.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When a gymnast's remains are found in a park, Booth and Brennan discover she was living a double life; Cam confronts the woman who stole her identity.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates when the remains of a girl who died 18 years earlier are delivered to Brennan and Booth's house; Brennan dreams about Pelant.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a country music singer whose remains were found in a shallow grave; Brennan takes a look at Wendall's injuries after he breaks his arm during a hockey game.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When the team discovers a renowned chess master in pig slop, Sweets goes under cover in the chess world; a science magazine honors Cam; Dr. Douglas Filmore fills in as lab intern.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a kidnap victim whose remains were found at a park; Hodgins makes a discovery about his family; Brennan and Boothe consider co-mingling their money.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Booth and CIA agent Danny Beck (Freddie Prinze Jr.) must track down a terrorist after a CIA informant is murdered; Brennan and Booth must consider the ramifications when Brennan wants to do more work in the field with Booth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When Stella's more famous sister returns after a 40-year family rift, the killings escalate, each of them reminiscent of a movie plot. Is life - and death - imitating art?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, amateur astronomer Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. Barnaby and Jones soon discover intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the star-seeking community.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Harriet Farmer wakes up after being in a coma since the night she tried to elope. Soon afterwards, a killer strikes members of a chess club. Could the attack which left Harriet for dead a year ago be linked to moves on a chess board? And where is her boyfriend Finn?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A woman is crushed to death with a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue. Secret and controversial plans to modernise the dairy are revealed while rumours about private lives of the parents cause a row at the prestigious local prep school. As other people linked to the dairy meet agonising deaths, long-held secrets start to emerge...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer is buzzing with excitement at the arrival of the annual ‘Paramount Dance Extravaganza’. But behind the sequins and smiles are deep running feuds and passions.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Prolific real estate agent Alexander Beauvoisin is murdered in front of a crowd at the unveiling of a new dolls house collection at Midsomer Museum of the family.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Deddington’s thriving bee Empire has put Granville Norton on the map, but what lengths will they go to to ensure they’re never dethroned…

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Fishermen and women flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As the Barnabys prepare for Cully's wedding, Lord Fitzroy and Beth Porteous are married in the pretty village of Bledlow. At the lavish reception at Bledlow Hall that follows, however, tragedy strikes - maid of honour Marina Fellowes is found murdered in one of the rooms by some of the guests. With their honeymoon cancelled, the Fitzroys must contend with a police presence in their home, as Barnaby and Jones try to learn more about the family history. It soon becomes clear there are secrets, deception, and an illegtimate son involved in the mystery, which is further complicated when the estate manager is killed by an arrow.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a good day for the Hicks family, as the name of Tommy Hicks - who was executed during World War I for "cowardice and desertion" - is added to the list on Midsomer Parva's war memorial. Tommy's son Lionel and grandson Dave, mayor of Causton and whom Barnaby does not like (owing to dodgy building work by the mayor's company), are thrilled, but the snooty Hammonds are decidedly less pleased. Henry, the patriarch of the Hammond family, is particularly riled by his insalubrious neighbours, and has nurtured a long-running feud with the Hickses. But the evening after a battle re-enactment in the village, Henry is murdered - and Barnaby must delve into the history of the two families to find the killer, discovering a web of fraud, lies, and hidden truths.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are in Dunstan to investigate the deaths of a reclusive couple. As they try to establish whether Ron and Libby Wilson were murdered or died of natural causes, their attention is drawn to a dispute raging between a local builder and road protesters nearby. When another death occurs, the detectives learn that there are plenty of dark secrets and fierce hostilities in this apparently idyllic village. Will Jack Purdy's quick temper lead to violence? How far would the protesters go to prevent the bypass being built? And do current events somehow relate to the death of Michael Wilson many years earlier?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the body of Londoner Charlie Finleyson is discovered in woodland near Midsomer Sonning, Barnaby and Jones find themselves investigating employees at local Midsomer Life magazine. It turns out the dead man was married to the ex-wife of Guy Sandys, the wealthy owner of the publication. As the detectives become aware of a feud between Guy and the proprietor of the Morecroft Hotel, tensions are also heating up between the locals and visiting Londoners. Soon Barnaby and Jones find themselves to trying to piece together the truth about the murder, and learn that someone might be trying to conceal something in their past.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death during a magic show in Midsomer Oaks, Barnaby and Nelson uncover conflict between the village church and ancient pagan traditions. Is famous magician Gideon Latimer to blame or is he the target?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are forced to take part in a team building exercise and Tom has plenty of problems dealing with the young, new, acting Chief Superintendent, John Cotton (Nick Fletcher). Following an explosion in a truck that had been stored in a truck depot, a body is soon discovered in a nearby lake, which turns out to be Alec Grainger, who had been seen arguing with James Parkes (Joseph Millson), the son of Colonel Matt Parkes (Tim Pigott-Smith). James appears to be involved in shady business deals and is blamed by his grandmother Caroline Halsey (Judy Parfitt) - and others - for the suicide of a woman he got pregnant two years before. Barnaby and Jones find themselves delving into a mixture of fraud, smuggling, and lies, trying to piece together the truth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When two couples vanish into thin air in the village of Monks Barton, Barnaby and Jones hear rumours of haunted woods and witchcraft. Barnaby remains sceptical of this, even when an unidentified body is found in a shallow grave and the missing start turning up dead. He soon has to determine if ghosts, criminals, or something else, is behind the abductions and murders.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. But when Sonia Woodley is stabbed in the churchyard two years after the death of her abusive husband Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, trying to work out who killed her. It soon becomes clear someone took a pair of tailor scissors to do the deed, but who amongst the villagers could have done so, is unclear, and the investigation is complicated, when the pair learn the local Anglican priest, the parish vicar, had received a letter from Sonia. When he himself is killed, the pair believe something in the letter may be key to solving the mystery.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is not convinced about Mayor David Hicks' plans to revive an annual trip to Brighton, suspecting his proposal to buy coastal land there, is rather a fraudulent scheme. Naturally, he joins the trip, but during their time in Brighton, a hated property developer (Tim McInnerny) is beheaded on a ghost house ride, prompting Barnaby to team up with his cousin to determine whether the developer's renters, the mayor, a gloomy reverend (Mark Gatiss), or the lady of a large estate are responsible, and soon discovers a hotbed of jealously, debt, lies and more, conceal a far darker secret.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Ford Florey is a town with a Wild West Society and many grudges. During a Wild West show at the local faire, the witch on the 'Dunk the Witch' stall is well and truly dunked but laughter soon turns to horror when she doesn't get up and the water in the tank starts to turn red. Barnaby and Jones need to be quick on the draw to track down the murderer, and soon begin to wonder whether a dispute over land was the motive, or something far more sinister, especially when more people are killed, with methods linked to the Wild West.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: On a dark night in the village of March Magna, Barnaby's wife Joyce swerves her car and narrowly misses a shadowy figure in the road - or so she thinks, when later that night a body is discovered in the old cemetery. The victim was a member of the council who was strange and weird, but someone seemed to have quite a grudge on him, and so Barnaby and Jones delve into his background, and the village, attempting to determine who might have wanted him dead, finding ghosts, grudges, and more beneath the village's surface.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Piano student Zoe Stock has won a place at the Devington Manor Winter School led by internationally renowned musician, Sir Michael Fielding (James Fox). Yet when she is there, things become mysterious when from the river bank in the grounds of the manor she sees a woman jump from the bridge and disappear underwater. When Barnaby and Jones start investigating the possible drowning, they discover unsavoury connections to the past that could prove lethal 20 years later, especially when someone attempts to kill. It isn't long, before murder does occur, and the detectives attempt to fathom what occurred in the drowning, and the present day murders.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Midsomer Morchard's very own boxer, John Kinsella, wins a world title in New York, the success seems to be a joyous time for all, until murder rears its ugly head. When a local solicitor is found dead by his assistant and Kinsella's manager, Barnaby attempts to determine what is going on, and soon discover issues with inheritance, affairs, anger, and gambling to make matters difficult. Meanwhile Kinsella's trainer Teddy Molloy (Phil Daniels) has his own hidden agenda. When a re-enactment of a Victorian boxing match gets heated, and Kinsella's manager is found dead in his home's gym, the pair attempt to determine who is responsible, although Barnaby's judgement seems to be clouded on this one.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute turns to murder, after a leading light in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe - but when a member of the possible scam is themselves murdered, Barnaby wonders what is truly going on behind the scenes of an important by-election.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby reluctantly accompanies Joyce on a spa weekend to Swavely Manor. But as he attempts to de-stress, a woman is found dead in the flotation chamber. He abandons his treatment to investigate, but his personal worries are never far away as he contemplates his future, while trying to determine if an argument between the spa's owners and their neighbours is the motive, or something else.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new Forensic Pathologist Kam Karimore are drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching as they seek to identify the villain.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Mysterious lights are seen in the sky over famous UFO hot-spot Cooper Hill. When a local forest-ranger suffers a strange death the visiting UFO spotters are convinced aliens are responsible. DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets in their search for the truth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle. When the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson find themselves drawn into a ruthlessly competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed where winning comes at any cost.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the picturesque Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new Sculpture Park. But when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson have to get creative to crack a case where art imitates death, and everything has a deeper meaning.

programme.title: Wardens

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On Sept. 13, 1982, Princess Grace of Monaco drove her old Rover 3500 car off the edge of a mountain road above the famous principality, she died in a hospital the following day, aged just 52.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Mary Tyler Moore, considered the sweetheart of American television, stars in two of the most successful sitcoms in history; behind her iconic smile lies a life beset by tragedy, illness and addiction.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: As the adorable Buffy in the 1960s sitcom "Family Affair," Anissa Jones was one of the most famous child stars of her generation; forensic pathologist Dr. Michael Hunter investigates her life and last hours to solve the mystery of her tragic death.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On March 29th 2016, the world is saddened by the news that Patty Duke has died; as Hollywood's original child star Duke had been charming audiences for over 60 years.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Desi Arnaz was famed for playing the much-loved character, Ricky Ricardo, in the 1950s TV series "I Love Lucy;" 30 years later, in the early hours of December 2nd, 1986, Desi died; he was 69 years old.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On June 25, 2009, actress and 1970s icon Farrah Fawcett dies of a rare form of cancer in Los Angeles; her death shocks her fans, friends and family as Farrah didn't smoke, ate healthy food and worked hard to keep fit.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Carrie Fisher died on December 27th 2016, four days after losing consciousness on a transatlantic flight; she was the daughter of Hollywood royalty, best known for playing Princess Leia in the "Star Wars" films.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On Dec. 28, 2016, the world is shocked when Hollywood icon Debbie Reynolds dies, just one day after the death of her daughter, actress and writer Carrie Fisher.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Elizabeth Taylor's life had been plagued by ill health, tragedy and failed marriages leading to addictions to alcohol and prescription drugs.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On the evening of January 20, 1993, actress and style icon Audrey Hepburn died of a very rare form of abdominal cancer, at the age of 63.

programme.title: Bobbi Kristina
programme.desc: The untold story of Bobbi Kristina Brown is one of adventure, dreams, conflict, perseverance, faith, friendship, and love � a love between mother and daughter. A love often interrupted by success, emotional upheaval and drug addiction but an unshakeable, incomparable, unwavering love nonetheless.

programme.title: The Rickey Smiley Show
programme.desc: Brandon dreads his first step-off. Kenny makes Simone jealous when he begins dating a sophisticated woman.

programme.title: The Rickey Smiley Show
programme.desc: Rickey goes up against Kenny and Maurice in a prank off that ends with disastrous results. When De'Anna gets dumped, Aunt Sylvia and Simone throw her a pamper party that's crashed by Juicy.

programme.desc: A team building exercise for the radio crew; Malik and Craig study and get dance lessons. Brandon plans an anniversary party.

programme.desc: Malik's school grades cause trouble. Rickey and Aaryn have a father-daughter talk. Craig and Malik embark on a business.

programme.desc: Erica and Wozy celebrate their birthdays together but Warryn misfires with a string of lame birthday presents to Erica or perhaps that�s all part of the plan. Joi records a music video for her new single, Coco Butter.

programme.title: The Manns
programme.desc: While Chantal and David SR faceoff in a family gumbo showdown, Porcia convinces David JR to buy her a gun, but only if she passes all of his tests. Tamela has only a few days to put on a fashion show to kick-start her clothing line.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Marques Houston joined the group Immature at the age of eight. That led to an extensive career with 13 albums and starring roles in hit movies and tv shows. He isuncensored as he recalls his 30 year journey as a multi-talented entertainer.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Emmy Award winning actress Sheryl Lee Ralph shines every week on the hit series Abbott Elementary, but this original Dreamgirl was a star way before that. She�suncensored as she walks us through her illustrious career from Broadway to Hollywood.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Kenny Lattimore helped spark an R&B revival in the mid ‘90s with a smooth sound and a look to match. But a rocky divorce from fellow star Chante Moore eventually overshadowed his career.

programme.title: The Bobby Debarge Story
programme.desc: BOBBY DEBARGE, lead singer of the 70�s R&B/Funk Band SWITCH, and eldest sibling of the world-famous pop group �DEBARGE� finds his life in peril as he struggles with fame and fortune while coping with the memories of his dysfunctional childhood.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Six-time Grammy Award winning gospel artist BeBe Winans is one of the industry’s first true crossover artists. He’s best known for his work as a gospel duo with his sister, buthis talents are seen across genres in music and on screen.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Grammy Award winning producer and rapper Swizz Beatz has been known for his music since he was a child. He was just a teenager when he began producing hitrecords for the Ruff Ryders music label, including Eve, The Lox, and his close friend DMX.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Freddie Jackson�s soulful ballads are the stuff of velvet sheets, intimate encounters and rose petaled Jacuzzis. With nine number one hits, including �Rock Me Tonight (For Old Times Sake)� and �You Are My Lady�, Freddie gave voice to sentiments men often struggled to communicate, and women longed to hear. But super-stardom wasn�t all strawberries and whipped cream. Struggling with his weight since childhood, Freddie found his persona at odds with his ballooning figure, while whispers questioning his sexuality swirled amongst fans. Through the 1980s, Freddie helped catapult the Hush productions sound to the R&B forefront - but when the hits ran out, he found himself facing financial ruin. In this revealing episode of �Unsung�, Freddie and his closest collaborators, including Melba Moore and M�lissa Morgan, chart his popular success and his personal struggles.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Led by the �Father of the Falsetto,� Bobby DeBarge, Switch paved the way for the hit-making DeBarge family with soulful, sensual ballads. But addictions and egos brought their brotherhood to a bitter end.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: In an age of popular family singing groups, from the Jacksons to the Osmonds, the Sylvers were the largest and the liveliest of them all. Nine members strong, they became best known for their disco friendly hits �Boogie Fever� and �Hot Line�, which drew inevitable comparisons to the sound of the Jackson 5. But the Sylvers were performing together long before that. Even as young children, they made their mark on television as �The Little Angels�, and later scaled the charts with songs written by their oldest brother, Leon Sylvers III, from �Fool�s Paradise� to �Misdemeanor�, that showed off their musical range and uncanny family harmonies. But internal conflicts and fights with their management broke up the group at the height of their career, and in its aftermath some of their members fell prey to drink and drugs, with dire consequences. Yet through it all the Sylvers remain united as a family, finding strength in their trials, as they reveal their full story in this poignant episode of �Unsung.�

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Freddie Jackson�s soulful ballads are the stuff of velvet sheets, intimate encounters and rose petaled Jacuzzis. With nine number one hits, including �Rock Me Tonight (For Old Times Sake)� and �You Are My Lady�, Freddie gave voice to sentiments men often struggled to communicate, and women longed to hear. But super-stardom wasn�t all strawberries and whipped cream. Struggling with his weight since childhood, Freddie found his persona at odds with his ballooning figure, while whispers questioning his sexuality swirled amongst fans. Through the 1980s, Freddie helped catapult the Hush productions sound to the R&B forefront - but when the hits ran out, he found himself facing financial ruin. In this revealing episode of �Unsung�, Freddie and his closest collaborators, including Melba Moore and M�lissa Morgan, chart his popular success and his personal struggles.

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Tasked with building a custom bike for a disabled man, Danny and his team must figure out a way to tackle a tricky transformation.

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.desc: Danny builds an extra special car for a customer who wants something no one else has--a tribute car of the rare Yenko Nova.

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: After months of bribery, bickering and buffoonery, Roli's tiny electric car is complete. Will Danny and the guys be as excited about the finished product as he is?

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The Ax Men soar to new heights as a helicopter logging outfit enters the competition. At Pihl, Dustin tries to talk his father into coming back to work.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: At Rygaard, the bosses clash over greenhorn Brad. Meanwhile, at the Pihl team, Dwayne is forced to do the job of a greenhorn.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The mountain's men face the firing squad. Rygaard's greenhorn fights to keep his job, but the bosses may pull the plug before he gets his chance.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The mountain seeks its revenge. Weather traps R&R's helicopter pilot in the fog. Trees fight back against Pihl's timber fallers.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The tables are turned and the mountain's underdogs get their chance. Jimmy Smith finds the holy grail of underwater logs when he attempts to pull a dock out of the water.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: Dead weight must be shed. Inexperienced hook tender Jesse Browning, junior pilot Steve Smith, and greenhorn Brad Hewitt.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: New faces are welcomed to the fraternity of men known as Ax Men. Rygaard and Conner Aviation hope their new greenhorns fare better than the last class.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The gap between winners and losers is narrowing. Rygaard's new greenhorn battles to prove himself, while Conner Aviation's substitute pilot gets wild with the chopper.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: The final week of logging's toughest season begins. Impossible goals leave no room for error, as each crew races the clock.

programme.title: Ax Men
programme.desc: As the logging season winds to a close, the drama heats up. Conner fights off defeat, while the men of Pihl fight each other.

programme.desc: From mountaintop to river's bottom, six crews will take on one of America's deadliest jobs.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: A family that spent 40 years building its very own castle by hand; a circus collector with a wax museum in his living room.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Ohio, a late friend's picking nirvana has the Wolfe brothers howling.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: At a gas station transformed into an eccentric home, Mike and Frank explore a collection that sends chills up their spines.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit a hot-rod mechanic with an awesome collection of bubbletop cars and automobilia.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys uncover a former firefighter's stockpile which includes a rare piece of space-age history; Frank's plan goes up in smoke.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A custom Fender quad guitar; a Wheaties box featuring Tonya Harding; two steelyard scales from the 1700s.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A 1974 Triumph Convertible; a pair of whale spears; a pair of Popeye water pistols.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Corey negotiates for an early 1900s Peerless Harvard dental chair; Rick looks at "Wayne's World" movie memorabilia and gets letters from a secret admirer.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A limited edition 2014 Hertz Penske GT Mustang races around the Las Vegas Motor Speedway; a 1600s lobster tail helmet from Oliver Cromwell's army comes into the shop; an old fashion apple peeler.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A pair of Duck's Foot pistols sends the guys off to the gun range; a bar of Yellow Kid soap from an 1896 newspaper comic; a Victorian-era hand crank.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A 1894 S Barber Dime comes into the shop; a John Belushi autograph; a 1950s Jim Henson; a 1965 Schwinn bicycle.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A Superman vs. Muhammad Ali comic book; an Edward Hopper charcoal piece; Rick feels that all the office phones are broken.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: An ultra-rare Nepalese Bira gun from the late 1800s comes into the shop; a 1970s Bob Peak TWA mural; a three-trumpet brass horn blares in.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: After test-driving the infamous O.J. Simpson Bronco, Rick must decide whether to purchase this million-dollar item; a rare silverware set; a 1978 "Star Wars" comic.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick drives to Los Angeles to see legendary baseball player Babe Ruth's "called shot" bat; Corey is enthused by a miniature model of Ralph Lauren's Bugatti Type 57SC.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A WWE Hall of Fame ring is brought into the shop; Corey checks out a 1927 Essex Sedan rat rod; an 1891 $1 silver certificate.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A 1965 Panama Canal service medal is presented to the shop; Corey sets his sights on a renaissance era crossbow; Chumlee finds himself in hot water after he fails to call in an expert.

programme.desc: Rick visits his friend's brewery in San Diego; antique beer items; antique an original McCormick Farmall tractor; a signed movie slate from the movie, "Django Unchained;" a "Dogs Playing Poker" print.

programme.desc: Rick drives to Los Angeles to see legendary baseball player Babe Ruth's "called shot" bat; Corey is enthused by a miniature model of Ralph Lauren's Bugatti Type 57SC.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: In this special episode, the Duck Commander team surprises two wounded veterans with a trip to Monroe so they can be in an episode of Duck Dynasty. To show their support, the Robertson family stages a "color run" to bring awareness to our wounded soldiers.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Alan agree to help Miss Kay open her own bakery, but things take a turn when it Kay is asked to hand over her secret recipes.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Missy and Jase's son Reed purchases an old van to boost his music career, his parents begin to question some of his choices.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie and his brothers spend too much time frog hunting, their wives challenge them to a "frogging" competition.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family takes a road trip to Liberty University where they will drop off John Luke and Mary Kate for their first year of college. Along the way, they stop in Nashville to visit Willie's friend Colt Ford.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family throws a party for Willie & Korie's 25th anniversary, but things take a dramatic turn when Willie and Korie reveal a big secret. Meanwhile, when Jase blames Uncle Si for getting him a parking ticket.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie asks a local chainsaw artist to make him a statue, he gets a big surprise when it's revealed that the statue is of an unlikely subject -- Willie himself. Meanwhile, Jep and Jessica attempt to help Kay with her hoarding.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: On a trip to New York, Jase's son Reed proposes to his girlfriend, which inspires his father to fan the flames of romance back home with Missy.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jase and Phil make a bet about whether or not human beings in a duck blind are visible from the air, Jep buys a drone copter to get a "bird's eye" view, but ends up losing it in the process.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase and the guys convince Willie to hire his older brother Alan to work at Duck Commander, but when Alan begins micromanaging everyone, they quickly change their mind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When the Robertsons throw a Renaissance Fair birthday party for Godwin, various family members compete in a tournament to win knighthood.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The tables are turned when the HOA comes to Jase for help to rid the neighborhood of a menacing beaver.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jase and Jep get into an argument over which of them is the rightful heir to their grandfather's old rifle, they decide to settle the argument with a marksmanship contest.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: After Si has started moonlighting as a grocery bagger, the guys show up at the store and their teasing inadvertently causes Si to lose his job and his chance to enter a local bagging competition.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When a large storm hits Monroe, most of the adults head to Phil and Kay's to help protect their house from flooding while Jess, Sadie, Rebecca and Cole babysit the kids.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie accompanies Kay to a local bingo tournament and gets seduced by the game, eventually embarrassing himself and Kay with his enthusiasm.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie takes Sadie on a tour of a local college in hopes of convincing her to stay nearby after her impending high school graduation.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When the Robertson brothers debate which of their wives makes the best cornbread, it escalates into a full-on cornbread cook off. Meanwhile, when Jep, Martin, and Godwin play a practical joke on Uncle Si.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The guys enjoy a one-day rodeo fantasy camp where they learn the ins and outs of riding, roping and clowning.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: After leaving school to pursue music, Jase feels that Reed needs some structure in his life, so he hires him to take his place at Duck Commander.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase accompanies Willie on a business trip to Arkansas to pitch a new product and has unorthodox sales strategies. Back in Monroe, Jep and Si have to track down an important item of Willie's that they accidentally shipped out.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie thinks his kids have been spending too much time on their phones and other devices so he initiates a "No-Tech Week," but when he has to participate too it ends up being harder than he thought.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Jase run against each other to become president of their neighborhood HOA. Jep, Jess and Miss Kay work on a Duck Commander themed display for the local zoo.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie buys a 3D printer for the office that starts off as a fun distraction for the guys until it appears the printer could soon replace their jobs. Si, upset that Willie invested in the 3D printer and didn't give him a raise, invests in John Luke's Sno­Cone stand.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase, Jep, Si and Cole try to fight back a growing population of cottonmouth snakes on Phil's property. Meanwhile, Willie uses his business expertise to help Rebecca improve her clothing store.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie puts Little Will on a budget for his first car but it backfires when Will decides to get a motorcycle instead.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: While many of the guys' wives and children are in Africa doing charity work, Willie and Jase get into a debate over tracking ability which leads to a challenge--can Jase and Si track and capture Willie and Jep?

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: With Korie still in Africa, Willie and Jep must step in and supervise Bella's sleepover. When a couple boys play a prank on the girls, Willie and Jep prove to be the perfect chaperones for the task.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Korie challenges Willie to come up with a romantic gesture without spending any money. Missy and Jase compete to see who can find a girlfriend for Mountain Man.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase's hopes to win the inaugural Duck Commander Fishing Tournament is buoyed when he is able to enlist the help of legendary angler Bill Dance. Meanwhile, Willie's vanity over his greying beard leads him to make a questionable decision.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase and Jep make a deal with Mia and Merritt to get them to go dove hunting with them. Si tries to make some extra cash by starting a West Monroe tour.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jumping Asian carp have invaded Monroe's waters so Jase and the guys set out to try to stop them from spreading further. Meanwhile, Willie and Si have to run Willie's Duck Diner for the day.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The family spends a weekend at their childhood summer camp, reliving old memories and working to repair a tennis court Willie built twenty years ago. While at the camp, Jep tries to pass a lifeguard swimming test he failed as a teenager.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Rowdy's adoption is finalized and he officially becomes a Robertson. Jase gets his first massage and enjoys it more than he expected.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: After Willie and Jase lose Si's tea cup, Si makes them go to great lengths to find it. When the entertainment for Gus's 1st birthday party falls through, Jep fills in as a magician.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Once in Montrose, Dog discovers that the million dollar fugitive is not the young bondsman's only problem. His kind heart and inexperience have led him to bail out a bevy of bail jumpers.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Can Dog complete his mission to clean-up this frontier town? With four outlaws still on the lam, Dog sets out to bring 'em in. As day turns into night and with the darkness and the danger mounting, an undaunted Dog and team plan late night hits.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today the cameras are turned around.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: In Hawaii, Dog sets off from Honolulu to Kona to help his son Leland with some of his fugitives. First, a stop at Leland's boxing gym to warm up for their day of hunting with a quick sparring session and some father-and-son time together.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Working for a bondswoman friend, Mary Ellen, Dog and his team hunt Chris, a fugitive wanted on drug charges. Dog turns over every rock, but to no avail.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What better way for Dog to start his morning, than at an elementary school Career Day, inspiring a new generation of potential bounty hunters? After giving a rousing speech to an audience of enthusiastic grade-schoolers, Dog catches up with an old friend.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is going to be the keynote speaker at the annual Profession Bail Agents convention in Las Vegas, but before he leaves he's got to clear two cases from his docket--Heidi and Stephanie. Heidi is a check forger who's failed to make a court appearance.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and his team fight the war on ice - crystal methamphetamine - on many fronts. They appear on a special anti-drug float in a parade. Then they go after bail-jumping Pana, a convicted drug dealer.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog visits his mother's grave for inspiration before heading out to find Frankie, a violent fugitive hiding in the jungle.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: A small town bondsmen is in over his head, his business on the line, when he makes a desperate plea for help. Dog agrees and launches a high stake hunt through the desolate and dangerous mountain landscape of western Colorado.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Once in Montrose, Dog discovers that the million dollar fugitive is not the young bondsman's only problem. His kind heart and inexperience have led him to bail out a bevy of bail jumpers.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Can Dog complete his mission to clean-up this frontier town? With four outlaws still on the lam, Dog sets out to bring 'em in. As day turns into night and with the darkness and the danger mounting, an undaunted Dog and team plan late night hits.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today the cameras are turned around.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: In Hawaii, Dog sets off from Honolulu to Kona to help his son Leland with some of his fugitives. First, a stop at Leland's boxing gym to warm up for their day of hunting with a quick sparring session and some father-and-son time together.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Working for a bondswoman friend, Mary Ellen, Dog and his team hunt Chris, a fugitive wanted on drug charges. Dog turns over every rock, but to no avail.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What better way for Dog to start his morning, than at an elementary school Career Day, inspiring a new generation of potential bounty hunters? After giving a rousing speech to an audience of enthusiastic grade-schoolers, Dog catches up with an old friend.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is going to be the keynote speaker at the annual Profession Bail Agents convention in Las Vegas, but before he leaves he's got to clear two cases from his docket--Heidi and Stephanie. Heidi is a check forger who's failed to make a court appearance.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Facing hard time for forging checks, Melissa pulled a vanishing act at her sentencing hearing, bolting from the courthouse in broad daylight. Lucky for her bondsman, Dog and his posse are in Colorado and ready to help.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: A Hawaiian surfing hero is on a wave to nowhere and it's Dog's turn to play lifeguard. "Buttons," known throughout the islands for his prowess on the waves and his daring 360's, has wiped-out on the rocky shoals of drug addiction.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's got a twofer on his hands, but the cases couldn't be more different. The first is a woman whose family wants her back in jail, the other is a former choirboy whose family ties are strong.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Roger has 27 prior arrests, spent years in jail and doesn't want to go back. But he's missed court and now Dog has to bring him in.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Kolani's on the run, left his wife to look after four kids and left Dog holding the bag for the $10k bond. Apparently the lure of the streets and other women have drawn him away from his commitments.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: The bounty-hunter adventures of Duane "Dog" Chapman and his Posse are chronicled in this one hour "Best Of" special, featuring superlative memorable moments from the previous seven seasons.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today the crew is hunting for Holly, a car thief. She's one of Tim's bonds and the entire Dog crew knows that means trouble--or at least a long and difficult chase.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is tracking down Cecilia, who's wanted for aggravated car theft, but she's nowhere to be found.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Colorado bondsman Bobby Brown's got trouble and his name is Jackson. Weighing in at over 240lbs and standing six foot tall this bad boy has skipped out on a $10,000 bond.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Back in Denver Dog discovers that recapturing the past can be more difficult then tracking down felons. On a day of more misses then hits in their fugitive hunting, Dog and Beth repeatedly collide with reminders of their early days.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Before Tim heads out for a tough day of bounty hunting, he likes to get in a little quality time with his daughter, Summer Rain. This morning, he takes the toddler for a spin around the yard in her little red wagon.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog leads the team on another adrenaline-filled chase in Denver. The fugitive, Robert, has a habit of address-hopping that makes him a difficult target, but Dog refuses to give up and finally tracks Robert down in his latest hideaway.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog, Beth and the team are in Colorado and today they face a special Chapman challenge--getting the kids to camp. After the drop-off, Dog is more than ready to track down a fugitive.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Michael is out on bail for drug sales, theft, and violation of parole. Dog's after him for failing to appear in court and his only lead is Jen, his co-signer and girlfriend.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog knows that it is better to give than to receive, so when a fellow bondsman grieving for his recently departed spouse requests his help, he jumps into action. Studying the fugitive's file the team uncovers a surprising fact.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today the team isn't pulling any punches, literally or figuratively! In between tracking down a potentially dangerous ex-con facing a five year stretch in the big house, Leland is struggling to find time to train.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team are back in Colorado hunting bounties. Today's fugitive has a long rap sheet and a reputation for packing heat. With nothing but an address and phone number, the team heads out on the hunt.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog told him not to go, but Charles went anyway, skipping to Oregon against the terms of his bail. Now Dog has a tip that Charles plans to slip back into Honolulu. With $30,000 on the line Dog knows he can leave little to chance.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is always trying to reduce the number of fugitives in Hawaii, so he really doesn't like felons from the mainland combining their flight from the law with an island vacation.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: "When you don't write the bond, you don't know the prey," as Dog always says. So when Dog and team help out a bondsman friend whose client jumped bail, the target is a blank slate. Are his tattoos gang related? Is he likely to be violent?

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: With twelve kids of his own Dog knows how hard it is to keep children on the straight and narrow, especially during their turbulent teens.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog knows that crime and drugs not only damage victims but also harm the families of the perpetrators, especially the kids. And both of the fugitives he's chasing today have left shattered families and struggling women in their wake.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: The "Big Island" of Hawaii boasts both lush tropical splendor and stark volcanic beauty. But today Dog and the team can't spend much time taking in the sights, they need to help Leland hunt down some elusive fugitives.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog doesn't know it yet, but today's bounty is about to take him on a trip down memory lane and test his investigative chops. With each lead propelling him closer and closer to his childhood haunts, Dog uses old-fashioned detective skills.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Cecily has graduated from High School and Dog believes the time is right to show her what the family business is all about. The rough and tumble world of bounty hunting is very different than the all girls private school.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog, Beth and the team are in Colorado and today they face a special Chapman challenge--getting the kids to camp. After the drop-off, Dog is more than ready to track down a fugitive.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Stories of civilizations large and small disappearing without a trace can be found throughout history and across the globe.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Who are the beings with big heads and giant eyes that seem to have been visiting our planet for millennia? Known by researchers as the "greys,".

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Specific regions of the world that are off-limits to exploration due to warfare, instability and inaccessibility.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigating the possible connection between powerful rulers of the past and extraterrestrial forces.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: If ancient aliens visited Earth, were they responsible for catastrophes, wars and other deadly disasters to control the fate of the human race?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Examining the unique geology and location of Mount Shasta in northern California, to determine why it is known as a hotbed of strange events, from Big Foot sightings to UFO encounters to hikers vanishing without a trace.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists investigate the mystery of the human genome and ponder the possibility that extraterrestrials aided in the survival of mankind from its earliest days.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Myths and legends have long been regarded as fantastic stories that describe powerful gods, mutant giants and fearsome monsters. But why do so many different cultures, separated by vast distances, tell the same stories?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Unearthly visitations... Heavenly messengers... Close encounters... Accounts of humans interacting with celestial beings have existed since the beginning of mankind.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists speculate about metal artifacts that could provide physical evidence of extraterrestrials visiting Earth in the distant past; in Jacksonville, Fla. a strange metallic sphere falls from the sky; an ancient aluminum wedge baffles historians.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Archaeologists and anthropologists make discoveries that receive no notice because they don't fit into the accepted scientific paradigm, such as an ancient hammer found in Texas or elongated skulls found on nearly every continent.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Exploring whether ancient accounts of "Star Ancestors" reveal profound truths about both humanity's origins and its future.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Japanese legend states that the Ishi-no-Hoden megalith, carved from a single rock and weighing roughly 500 tons, was built to depict a sky ship made of stone that the ancient people witnessed descending from the heavens.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In 2017, news breaks that the Department of Defense recently spent millions investigating reports of UFOs; the latest disclosure about a series of secret government UFO projects dating back decades.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists speculate about the existence of black holes on Earth, which may explain strange disappearances and other inexplicable phenomena.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In the nearly 2,000-year-old text called "The Zohar," there is a curious passage that describes hidden realms beneath the surface of the Earth, each of which is said to be populated by both human and non-human intelligences.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: According to some Ancient Astronaut theorists, entities that have been described as ghosts, angels and extraterrestrials are actually visitors from unseen realms and they walk on Earth.

programme.title: Nightwatch
programme.desc: Every night, the members of the Nightwatch in New Orleans are reminded in new ways to be thankful for what they have.

programme.title: Nightwatch
programme.desc: The EMT's recall and reflect on their craziest and most memorable calls on the Nighwatch shift in New Orleans.

programme.title: Nightwatch
programme.desc: The EMT's recall and reflect on their craziest and most memorable calls on the Nighwatch shift in New Orleans.

programme.title: Nightwatch
programme.desc: It's the week of Mardi Gras and it's the busiest time of year for our first responders. Working the parade route comes with its own challenges and unique cases.

programme.title: Nightwatch
programme.desc: Swat officers J. and Alley come face-to-face with the violence in the city after a high-speed chase turns into a shootout with their suspect.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: March 24th, 2016. The Sheriff's Office in DeRidder, Louisiana receives a distressing phone call from the owner of Dee's Motel. The man tells them that he's found a woman dead in room 6.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: Police receive a distressing 911 call from Brian Redding. He is heard crying and screaming that he has found his 16-year-old girlfriend dead in their room at the Forest Motel in Ridgeland, South Carolina.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: August 29, 2006, Riverside County, CA. Investigators are shocked to discover the brutalized corpses of two men stuffed in the trunk of an abandoned vehicle.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: May 13, 2020, Reno, Nevada. Officers from the Reno Police Department are dispatched to the La Quinta Inn after several motel guests report hearing gunshots in the middle of the night.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: July 9th, 2018, police receive a distressing call from a woman claiming she and her friends have been kidnapped and terrorized at a Travelodge. One of them has sustained a fatal gunshot wound.

programme.title: Murder at the Motel
programme.desc: January 5, 2016, Baltimore, MD. Police officers respond to a report of a deceased man found at the Deluxe Plaza Motel in Northeast Baltimore. The victim, US Navy Veteran Edward Yesaitis, was discovered by the motel's cleaning crew.

programme.title: Beyond the Headlines: The Gabby Petito Story

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: The young woman confined to a wheelchair seemed helpless, but Gypsy Blanchard and her boyfriend were plotting murder.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: When Ohio State senior Reagan Tokes fails to respond to messages from her parents, they fear the worst; their nightmare becomes a reality when a caller reports the discovery of a female body in the local park; detectives race to solve the crime.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: A 13-year-old doesn't show up for a play date and is reported missing, setting off a manhunt in the rough Colorado wilderness; when his badly decomposed remains are found by hikers three years later, prosecutors charge the teen's father with murder.

programme.desc: Makeva Jenkins, a successful businesswoman and mother of three, boasted on Facebook that she had grown her business to six figures; hours later, she is shot dead with a bullet to the head.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: The rich, handsome boyfriend was everything Shayna Hubers dreamed of - so why did she pull the trigger?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria Marmolejo's attempt to stop her son's illicit relationship ended in her murder by him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Criminologists, forensic pathologists, federal agents, and other experts delve into the lives of the most terrifying and notorious killers in history.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, is a serial killer who mutilates and kills at least eight women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gardener William Howell used his van as a "murder mobile," killing women and dumping bodies.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stuart Campbell is convicted for the murder of his 15-year-old niece in 2002, even though her body has never been found.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A decade-old link revealed a serial killer who kept the same M.O. after moving far away.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Malcolm Green, who was jailed for life for murder, only to kill again when he was released.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the UK's most infamous serial killers, Joanna Dennehy brutally stabbed three men to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Criminologists, forensic pathologists, federal agents, and other experts delve into the lives of the most terrifying and notorious killers in history.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
